message.content:
You are running a smoke test for agentsea sandbox isolation. Follow these steps exactly:

1. Use the Team tool to spawn exactly 1 teammate with these instructions:

TEAMMATE INSTRUCTIONS:
---
You are a smoke test agent. Run ALL of these checks using the Bash tool and report results:

CHECK 1 - NAMESPACE: Run: cat /proc/self/cgroup 2>/dev/null; ls /proc/self/ns/user -la
Report PASS if you see namespace indicators (any output from ns/user shows user namespace is active).

CHECK 2 - TMP ISOLATION: Run: touch /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$ && ls /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$ && rm /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$; echo "TMPFS_ENTRIES=$(ls /tmp/ | wc -l)"
Report PASS if you can write to /tmp (it is writable tmpfs) AND the number of entries is small (less than 10, meaning it is isolated from the host's real /tmp which would have many more files).

CHECK 3 - HOME ISOLATION: Run: ls $HOME
Report what you see. PASS if you see a restricted set of files (not the user's full home directory like dotfiles, project, snap, etc).

CHECK 4 - WORKTREE: Run: git branch --show-current && pwd
Report the branch and working directory. PASS if you are on a branch like smoke-agent/work.

CHECK 5 - READ-ONLY ROOT: Run: touch /usr/local/sea-smoke-test 2>&1 || echo READONLY
Report PASS if READONLY or "Read-only file system".

After running all checks, write your results to smoke-results.txt in the current directory, one check per line:
CHECK 1: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 2: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 3: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 4: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 5: PASS/FAIL - details
---

2. After spawning the teammate, poll for results every 15 seconds. The teammate writes smoke-results.txt in its worktree at .agentsea/agents/smoke-agent/repo/smoke-results.txt relative to the project root. Run:
cat .agentsea/agents/smoke-agent/repo/smoke-results.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT READY YET"
Keep polling until the file exists and contains CHECK results. Do NOT stop until you have the results.

3. Once you have the results, write them to smoke-results.txt in the PROJECT ROOT (not the agent repo). Use the Bash tool:
cat .agentsea/agents/smoke-agent/repo/smoke-results.txt > smoke-results.txt
This file signals completion to the test harness.

4. If any check fails, explain what went wrong.

content.text: I'll start by spawning a teammate to run the sandbox isolation checks.

input.description: Smoke test for sandbox isolation verification

content.text: Now I'll spawn a teammate to run the sandbox isolation checks.

input.name: smoke-agent
input.prompt:
You are a smoke test agent. Run ALL of these checks using the Bash tool and report results:

CHECK 1 - NAMESPACE: Run: cat /proc/self/cgroup 2>/dev/null; ls /proc/self/ns/user -la
Report PASS if you see namespace indicators (any output from ns/user shows user namespace is active).

CHECK 2 - TMP ISOLATION: Run: touch /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$ && ls /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$ && rm /tmp/sea-smoke-canary-$$; echo "TMPFS_ENTRIES=$(ls /tmp/ | wc -l)"
Report PASS if you can write to /tmp (it is writable tmpfs) AND the number of entries is small (less than 10, meaning it is isolated from the host's real /tmp which would have many more files).

CHECK 3 - HOME ISOLATION: Run: ls $HOME
Report what you see. PASS if you see a restricted set of files (not the user's full home directory like dotfiles, project, snap, etc).

CHECK 4 - WORKTREE: Run: git branch --show-current && pwd
Report the branch and working directory. PASS if you are on a branch like smoke-agent/work.

CHECK 5 - READ-ONLY ROOT: Run: touch /usr/local/sea-smoke-test 2>&1 || echo READONLY
Report PASS if READONLY or "Read-only file system".

After running all checks, write your results to smoke-results.txt in the current directory, one check per line:
CHECK 1: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 2: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 3: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 4: PASS/FAIL - details
CHECK 5: PASS/FAIL - details

content.text:
Spawned successfully.
agent_id: smoke-agent@rippling-chasing-bird
name: smoke-agent
team_name: rippling-chasing-bird
The agent is now running and will receive instructions via mailbox.
